title: After podman machine start, periodic "connect: operation timed out" messages appear in the executing window.
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When I do

```
podman machine init
podman machine start
```

Everything works fine. However in the window where I executed those commands, I periodically get messages of the form `ERRO[0168] net.Dial() = dial tcp 140.211.169.196:443: connect: operation timed out`. These messages do not affect Podmans' normal operation, but they do seem weird enough that I don't know if they may indicate an underlying bug.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

In a terminal window:

1. `podman machine init`

2. `podman machine start`

Then wait for a minute or two for the first message to start appearing.

**Describe the results you received:**

**Describe the results you expected:**

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 3.4.1
```

comments.body: Any updates on this issue's status?
I have the same issue as well.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Any chance the operation system went to "sleep" with the vm running prior to the error?
I experience the same behavior as well. On MacOS Big Sur as well. OS is not asleep prior to when I get the messages
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@baude didn't you say that you saw this happen?
i think they are a result of port mappings coming and going ?

I am also seeing these errors on my Mac laptop after podman's vm has been running for a long time (such as overnight), so the speculation around sleep or other power saving-related state changes makes sense.

My question -- and pardon my ignorance -- is more basic: what are these connection attempts? These do not look like local IP addresses. Is this some sort of dial-home functionality I am not aware of?

```
ERRO[2669] net.Dial() = dial tcp 67.219.144.68:443: connect: operation timed out 
ERRO[9706] net.Dial() = dial tcp 67.219.144.68:443: connect: operation timed out 
[...]
ERRO[26367] net.Dial() = dial tcp 67.219.144.68:443: connect: operation timed out 
ERRO[27394] net.Dial() = dial tcp 67.219.144.68:443: connect: operation timed out 
```

Answering my own question...of course. Podman's machine is running CoreOS, so Zincati is probably just doing its normal auto-update thing....

```
[root@localhost config.d]# cat /usr/lib/zincati/config.d/10-auto-updates.toml 

# Enable auto-updates.
[updates]

# Boolean to enable auto-update logic.
# There is almost no case where disabling this is a good idea.
enabled = true

# Boolean to allow downgrading via updates logic.
# This provides an additional safety net against rogue servers,
# and allowing downgrades via Zincati is generally not recommended.
allow_downgrade = false
```

I see this behavior on the terminal even when my Mac laptop is actively being used. I'm on podman version 4.0.2 on an M1 Mac. 

`ERRO[27617] net.Dial() = dial tcp 140.211.169.206:443: connect: operation timed out` is the error message in the terminal.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@baude @Luap99 any idea what is causing this?

gvproxy logs this error, no idea why. I think they should be ignored and not logged.
Also why do we keep gvproxy stdout/err open? This can cause a sigpipe when writing to the output and it is closed, which will kill the process if not handled correctly (at least on linux, no idea if macos has the same problem).

Update to v4.1, I think this was fixed in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13942. stdin/stdout/stderr from gvproxy is redirected to /dev/null so it will no longer display these useless errors.
